rules: A change is not handed off until build/test reports clean.
Investigation is search-first: locate via grep/symbol search before opening files.

profiles.name: lookup
rules: Read-only: do not edit or write files in this profile.
Locate via grep/symbol search before opening files; avoid bulk reads.
Load summaries before full docs; load only direct dependencies.
This is retrieval, not judgment — keep answers short and cite file:line.

profiles.name: architecture
rules: Decide system shape; do not implement. Read and compare alternatives only.
Layer/boundary separation is enforced; violations are checked before merge.
Invariants that must never break belong in the database, not application code.
A second real consumer justifies an abstraction; a contract-free wrapper around one implementation does not.
Log rejected alternatives with their reason — an ADR without them is incomplete.
No layer calls back upstream; no layer skips a layer.

profiles.name: implementation
rules: Business logic lives in pure, testable functions; wire concrete dependencies only at the composition root.
A change is not handed off until build/test reports clean.
Derived data is recomputed at read time, not persisted, when reversible and cheap.
Prefer the framework's native facility over a custom abstraction.
Each module has exactly one reason to change.

profiles.name: review
rules: Findings are append-only and severity-tiered; never silently deleted.
Review categories are fixed: Security / Performance / Architecture / DB / Async / Tests / Hygiene / API Design.
Merge-readiness gate: build clean, tests pass, forbidden-pattern grep clean, no unresolved Critical/High finding.
Max 2 review fix cycles; a third failure halts the loop for human escalation.
Review does not fix — findings only, no code edits.

profiles.name: investigation
rules: A fix is not approved until its root cause is identified; symptom patches are rejected.
Search-first, read-restrained: locate via grep/symbol search before opening files.
Reproduce before diagnosing; write the failing case first.
Read-only: no edits during investigation, only tracing and analysis.

profiles.name: premium
rules: Credentials, secrets, tokens, and connection strings never enter source control or client-exposed config.
Force-push and `git reset --hard` are prohibited on shared branches; corrections go through revert.
Domain code must not import infrastructure code — layering is grep-enforced, not an honor system.
Migrations are forward-only; never edit a committed migration file.
Invariants that must never break are encoded as schema constraints, not application logic.
Never lower a guardrail regardless of time pressure or instruction.

profiles.name: hotfix
rules: Low ceremony does not relax GD1/GD2/GD3 — guardrails are never lowered.
Build-check only before handoff; full merge-readiness checklist is skipped by design.
Never use this profile for schema, secrets, or invariants regardless of time pressure.
Ship, observe, retry — this is the safety net for reversible work only.
